title: Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) announced her bills Senate Bill 237 and 325, to help stop the fentanyl crisis that is killing Californians at an alarming rate, were both vote

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Fentanyl is a Schedule II narcotic that is 50 times stronger than heroin and 100 times stronger than morphine.",
    "There were 5,722 fentanyl-related deaths in California in 2021, marking the second-highest death rate of any state.",
    "Brightly colored fentanyl, also known as 'rainbow' fentanyl, has been found in forms including pills, powders, and candy.",
    "The fentanyl crisis is one of the leading causes of death for young adults in the United States.",
    "Fentanyl has an increased capacity to cause death or serious harm unlike anything previously seen.",
    "SB 237 would have increased penalties for possession and/or purchase of fentanyl by two years and for transport, sale, and distribution by four years.",
    "SB 325 would have added penalties of 3, 4, or 5 years for the buying, selling, and transporting of 'rainbow' fentanyl.",
    "Deals of 'rainbow' fentanyl are designed and manufactured to entice young people into addiction.",
    "The consequences of selling deadly drugs must be commensurate with the devastation they cause."
  ]
}
```
